text: The primary outcome occurred in 6.6% of patients in the semaglutide group and 8.9% in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.58 to 0.95; P=0.02).
The primary outcome occurred in fewer patients in the liraglutide group (hazard ratio, 0.87; 95% CI, 0.78 to 0.97; P=0.01).
MACE occurred in 12.0% of participants in the dulaglutide group versus 13.4% in the placebo group (hazard ratio 0.88, 95% CI 0.79-0.99; p=0.026).
The hazard ratio for MACE with oral semaglutide was 0.79 (95% CI, 0.57 to 1.11; P=0.17).
The hazard ratio for the primary outcome was 0.73 (95% CI, 0.58 to 0.92; P=0.007) with efpeglenatide.
The hazard ratio for the primary outcome was 0.91 (95% CI, 0.83 to 1.00; P=0.06) with exenatide.
The hazard ratio for MACE was 0.78 (95% CI 0.68-0.90; p=0.0006) with albiglutide.
The hazard ratio for MACE-4 was 0.74 (95% CI 0.51-1.08) with tirzepatide versus insulin glargine.
The mean change in body weight was -14.9% with semaglutide versus -2.4% with placebo (estimated treatment difference, -12.4 percentage points; 95% CI, -13.4 to -11.5; P<0.001).
The mean change in body weight was -20.9% with tirzepatide 15mg versus -3.1% with placebo (difference, -17.8 percentage points; 95% CI, -19.3 to -16.3).
A MACE event occurred in 569 patients (8.0%) in the semaglutide group and 701 (11.8%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.80; 95% CI, 0.72 to 0.90; P<0.001).
The hazard ratio for the primary kidney outcome was 0.76 (95% CI, 0.66 to 0.88; P=0.0003) with semaglutide.
The hazard ratio for the primary outcome was 0.72 (95% CI, 0.64 to 0.82; P<0.001) with empagliflozin.
The primary outcome occurred in 9.2% of participants in the dapagliflozin group and 14.5% in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.72; P<0.001).
The hazard ratio for metabolic failure was 0.74 (95% CI, 0.64 to 0.84) for liraglutide versus glimepiride.
